author: taulepton_
content: I'll be making updates to this branch this week and will keep this thread updated. <@824439091004309535> can you let me know how you plan to consume this for the zkAttester demo please?

author: fbwoolf
content: I was planning just to fork the repo and branch off the `optional-proofs` branch, does that work for you all?

author: taulepton_
content: yes that works. Is there a specific feature/function that you need that would require a fork?

author: fbwoolf
content: Since this is purely a demo and won’t be merged back, I figured forking + branching off optional-proofs is the simplest. I'll just be redoing the work from here: https://github.com/kevaundray/lighthouse/pull/17

author: taulepton_
content: Yes, I think forking could make sense, but I'm unsure if a fork will be needed. I'm trying to understand whether there are any code/feature changes you need to make that warrant a fork, or whether the optional-proofs branch already has all the functionality you need?

author: fbwoolf
content: I'll need to add code to make the demo work, so yes forking is best

author: taulepton_
content: Got it 🙂 I'll keep you informed as I update the branch this week and put together some high-level docs of how things piece together.
